data.title: Pfizer boosts anti-obesity efforts: names former J&J exec. as chief internal medicine officer
data.description:
• Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) has named James List, a former Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) executive, as the company’s new chief internal medicine officer to boost the company's efforts in obesity drug development.
  • The New York-based pharma giant said that List, who has done his post-doctoral research in the GLP-1 class of weight loss drugs, will oversee the company’s R&D work related to its upcoming cardiovascular, metabolic, hematologic, and obesity medicines.
  • List joins PFE from J&J’s (NYSE:JNJ) Janssen unit, where he served as global therapeutic area head for cardiovascular & metabolism.
  • The appointment comes as Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) is racing to grab a slice of the obesity drug market dominated by Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO), which, according to a recent research note from TD Cowen, are expected to maintain their duopoly until 2030.
  • In 2023, PFE discontinued a twice-daily regimen for its oral obesity therapy, danuglipron, citing side effects, and instead moved to advance a once-daily formulation into late-stage development.
  • At the recent earnings call, Pfizer’s (NYSE:PFE) chief scientific officer, Chris Boshoff, said that PFE remains on track to deliver a data update on the GLP-1 receptor agonist in Q1 2025.

data.title: Vertiv declares $0.0375 dividend
data.description:
• Vertiv (NYSE:VRT) declared $0.0375/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.18%

data.title: Textron rises after report says U.S. Army selected for combat vehicle program
data.description:
Textron (NYSE:TXT) +2.4% in Friday's trading after Breaking Defense reported the company's Ripsaw 3 program has won the U.S. Army's latest Robotic Combat Vehicle competition.

The official decision is not expected to be announced until later this month, but vendors have been notified, according to the report.

"The Ripsaw M3 leverages a modular open systems architecture design and common chassis to support a variety of interchangeable payloads from a basic flat-top deck configuration," Textron (NYSE:TXT) wrote in a press release last year. "A rugged and reliable RCV platform, the vehicle is designed to meet Army requirements while preserving transportability and mission versatility."

Textron's (TX) Ripsaw M5 secured the medium prototyping contract in an earlier iteration of the Robotic Combat Vehicle with three vehicle sizes, but the Army changed course and relaunched a new competition for just one RCV size.

data.title: MARA, Hut 8 land Buy ratings in new Rosenblatt coverage
data.description:
Rosenblatt analyst Chris Brendler initiated coverage on MARA Holdings (NASDAQ:MARA) stock with a Buy rating earlier in the week, calling it the "undisputed market leader" among publicly-traded bitcoin (BTC-USD) mining companies.

With a 6% market share that surpasses its closest competitor by 30%, MARA (NASDAQ:MARA) "has taken full advantage of its industry-leading scale," Brendler wrote in a note.

He added that "its Bitcoin (BTC-USD) HODL is almost larger than the next 10 peers combined, and its tech stack is unmatched."

Still, over the past year, the MARA (MARA) shared shed 33%, which Brendler attributed to "macro concerns about interest rates and its aggressive share issuance. MARA is among the most levered to our bullish Bitcoin views," yet bitcoin has climbed 34% over the same period.

In a separate note, Brendler gave a new Buy recommendation on Hut 8 Corp. (NASDAQ:HUT), as its recent merger with US Bitcoin is poised to "unlock significant value" for investors.

"Although the first year of integration has been impacted by restructuring, we believe 2025 starts the growthphase of this compelling combination," he wrote.

MARA gained 2.1%, while HUT slid 3.1%, in Friday morning trading.

Seeking Alpha's peer comparison page evaluates various metrics for the two BTC miners.

data.title: Volaris reports load factor of 85% for February 2025
data.description:
• Controladora Vuela Compañía de Aviación (NYSE:VLRS) on Friday reported that its ASM capacity increased by 3.4% year-over-year, while RPMs for the month grew by 2.5% in February.
  • Mexican domestic RPMs increased 3.6%, while international RPMs rose 0.7%. As a result, the load factor decreased by 0.7 percentage points year-over-year to 85.2%.
  • During the month, Volaris transported 2.2 million passengers.
  • VLRS shares were down nearly 2% to $6.14 during the afternoon trade on Friday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bank of America, JPMorgan, Huntington Bancorp upped at Baird
data.description:
Baird upgraded Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) and Huntington Bancorp (NASDAQ:HBAN) to Outperform and JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) to Neutral after a broad selloff in bank stocks resulted in an improved risk/reward relative to the broader market.

"While the group could remain volatile near term, we believe upside potential finally outweighs downside risk in several bank names as the election euphoria has faded and then some," analyst David A. George wrote in a note to clients.

Baird credits Bank of America's (NYSE:BAC) "best-in-class deposit franchise, consistent execution, relatively low credit risk, and top-tier markets businesses," but felt its valuation didn't "make sense." BAC now trades at 1.15x book, and 7-8x Baird's estimated through-the-cycle or normalized EPS estimate of $4.80, which George considers conservative.

Huntington Bancorp (NASDAQ:HBAN) "is positioned to generate above-average PPNR growth over the coming years, from NIM expansion, new market initiatives, along with the build-out of its fee businesses. Insider ownership is high, and management consistently behaves like owners," the analyst said. That makes HBAN's ~9x price/2026 EPS estimate multiple and ~4.1% dividend yield look attractive, he added.

JPMorgan Chase's (NYSE:JPM) current premium is warranted, given its best-in-class franchise, but is "too pricey for new money," George wrote. Thus, Baird upgraded the stock to Neutral from Underperform.

The SA Quant rating has Hold ratings on all three stocks, while the average Wall Street rating for each of them is Buy.

JPMorgan Chase (JPM) slid 2.3%, Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) dipped 1.9%, and Bank of America (BAC) edged down 0.2% in Friday morning trading.

data.title: DeepSeek app likely to be banned from U.S. gov't devices: report
data.description:
• DeepSeek's (DEEPSEEK) artificial intelligence app is likely to be banned from U.S. government devices amid national security concerns, The Wall Street Journal reported.
  • U.S. officials are worried about how the artificial intelligence lab handles user data, which is stored on servers in China, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. DeepSeek also has not yet given a strong enough explanation of what it does with the data it obtains from users and which parties have access to the data.
  • Other potential moves being weighed by the Trump administration include a ban from U.S. app stores and putting limits on cloud service providers offering DeepSeek's AI models.
  • DeepSeek did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Fed's Bowman says job market may play bigger role in FOMC policy talks
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman expects that the job market and overall economic activity will play a greater role in the Federal Open Market Committee's policy debates, she said on Friday, addressing research presented at a conference exploring how monetary policy affects economic activity. 

"Although the FOMC has been focused on lowering inflation in the past few years, as we continue to make progress on approaching our 2 percent target, I expect that the labor market and economic activity will become a larger factor in the FOMC's policy discussions," she said in remarks prepared for delivery at a University of Chicago Booth School of Business event in New York.

Bowman did not reference Friday's nonfarm payrolls report, which showed still-solid job growth last month.

Elsewhere in her remarks, the Fed governor said that the so-called neutral rate, also known as R-star, — the rate that neither stimulates nor slows growth — likely increased since the pandemic. That implies that borrowing costs may need to remain higher than before to keep the economy in balance.

“One way to describe the resiliency of real activity to higher interest rates during the recent tightening cycle is to say that some of the previously noted factors led to a rise in R-star,” she said.

data.title: Traders move eye toward June rate cut by Fed after February jobs report
data.description:
Traders on Friday kicked up odds of a June interest-rate cut by the Federal Reserve after markets received an overall stable U.S. jobs report for February. 

Traders in the fed futures market raised June rate-cut odds to 53.7% from 47.5% a day ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool. The May probability stepped down to 41.5% from 46.5% on Thursday. 

The “employment report highlighted the obvious in that the US economy is indeed slowing on the margin, but more importantly, we are not seeing the economy fall off a cliff,” Charlie Ripley, senior investment strategist at Allianz Investment Management, said in a note. 

“Overall, this report is a sigh of relief for the Fed as they can continue to sit on the sidelines for the next couple meetings as they assess the potential inflation impacts resulting from US tariff policy over the coming months,” Ripley said. 

Nonfarm payrolls rose 151K in February vs. 160K consensus. The unemployment rate edged up to 4.1% vs. 4.0% consensus. A cooler 0.3% increase in average hourly earnings matched the consensus. Federal employment was down 10K as U.S. President Donald Trump’s appointed DOGE team directed workforce cuts. 

“The Fed is in a pickle,” Comerica Bank Chief Economist Bill Adams said. “On the one hand, slower job growth and rising unemployment are arguments for a rate cut. On the other hand, inflationary policies like tariffs and immigration restrictions are arguments against a cut,” he said.

“Most likely the Fed will sit on their hands for a bit, just like private hiring managers seem to be doing," Adams said. 

In markets Friday, Treasury yields were lower. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 4 basis points to 4.23%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 4 basis points to 3.91%.

U.S. stocks (SP500)(NYSEARCA:SPY)(COMP:IND)(DJI) fell, with equities heading for weekly losses.

data.title: Eli Lilly posts long-term data for eczema therapy Ebglyss
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) on Friday said that its recently approved eczema therapy Ebglyss led to complete skin clearance in 50% of individuals who took part in a long-term extension study.

Citing data from ADjoin, a long-term extension study of the company's Phase 3 Ebglyss trials, the Indiana-based drugmaker said that 50% of trial subjects met the highest bar of therapeutic response, showing complete skin clearance as measured by a clinical score known as EASI 100.

Meanwhile, 87% achieved almost-clear skin (EASI 90) at three years. As for tolerability, the company said that the safety findings were in line with those from Ebglyss trials.

Those who responded to Ebglyss at Week 16 from the company’s ADvocate 1 and ADvocate 2 monotherapy trials took part in Adjoin. They received biweekly or once-monthly Ebglyss as maintenance therapy at a 250 mg dose.

Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) develops the injectable in partnership with Spanish biopharma company Almirall (OTCPK:LBTSF). In September, the FDA approved Ebglyss for use in those aged 12 years and older with moderate-to-severe eczema (atopic dermatitis) after topical prescription drugs.
